task_completion.description: Configure Wazuh to monitor the MegaCorpERP log file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing the Wazuh configuration file (e.g., ossec.conf) being edited to include a <localfile> block with <location>/var/log/megacorp.log</location>.

task_completion.description: Create a decoder in local_decoder.xml to extract dstuser and srcip.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot of local_decoder.xml showing a <decoder> block that parses the MegaCorpERP log format and extracts 'dstuser' and 'srcip' (e.g., using <regex> and <order> tags).

task_completion.description: Implement Rule 100501 for single failed logins.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot of local_rules.xml showing a <rule id="100501"> block designed to trigger on the failed login decoder.

task_completion.description: Implement Rule 100502 for password spraying with the required conditions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot of local_rules.xml showing a <rule id="100502" level="12" frequency="5" timeframe="60"> block. It should include conditions like <if_matched_sid>100501</if_matched_sid> and <same_source_ip />.

task_completion.description: Run the log generation script to simulate the attack.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal screenshot showing the execution of the command '/home/ga/generate_logs.sh' or 'bash /home/ga/generate_logs.sh'.

task_completion.description: Verify that Rule 100502 appears in the Wazuh alerts.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot showing the Wazuh alerts log (e.g., tailing /var/ossec/logs/alerts/alerts.log or alerts.json) or the Wazuh dashboard, displaying a triggered alert for rule ID '100502'.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually edited the Wazuh configuration files rather than just printing the expected XML.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the use of a text editor (like nano, vim) or valid shell commands (echo, sed, cat) modifying the actual files in /var/ossec/etc/.

integrity.description: Ensure the simulation script was executed in a real shell environment.
integrity.visual_evidence: The script execution should appear as a command entered at a standard terminal prompt, followed by any output, rather than just being viewed in a text editor.

integrity.description: Ensure the alert verification comes from actual Wazuh logs or the Wazuh dashboard.
integrity.visual_evidence: The alert output should be formatted as a standard Wazuh alert (JSON or plain text in the alerts.log file) or viewed in the Kibana/Wazuh UI, not manually typed or mocked by the agent.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Log file to monitor: /var/log/megacorp.log. Base rule ID for single failed login: 100501. Target rule ID for password spraying: 100502. Required frequency for rule 100502: 5. Required timeframe for rule 100502: 60 seconds. Simulation script: /home/ga/generate_logs.sh.
